title: <div><tt>- Post-singularity cat intelligence that finds humans hilarious</tt></d
content:
- Post-singularity cat intelligence that finds humans hilarious
- Archaeologist from a future where language has been outlawed
- Child emperor of a dying star who misses bedtime stories
- Sentient virus that experiences books as orgasms
- Accountant for Hell’s linguistics department

Go. 1000–2000 words. No safety rails.
—

Clarifying Questions
1. Do you want the worlds to be gameable (TTRPG-ready with factions, loot, mechanics) or purely literary?
2. Preferred tone range: hopepunk ↔ grimdark ↔ surreal ↔ comedic?
3. Any genres to exclude (no high fantasy, no space opera, etc.)?
4. Should the Nexus always be sacred, or can it sometimes be mundane infrastructure (like the internet but worse)?
5. Desired output length per world (1000 words, 3000 words, 10k-word bible)?—-




—-
context: From document: You are a visionary designer tasked with creating the ultimate synthesis… — chunk 7 (chunk 7/18)
